title: Protection of electrical components in solar energy shade structure
abstract: In accordance with various exemplary embodiments, solar energy shade structures and support systems are disclosed that have electrical components concealed or screened within columns located under the structure. For example, a solar energy structure may comprise: a solar panel support structure, a plurality of solar panels supported by the solar panel support structure, a plurality of vertical supports connected to the solar panel support structure for supporting the solar panel support structure elevated above a surface, and a column, located under the solar panel support structure. The column comprises an electrical component mounted to the column in a screened manner, wherein the electrical component comprises at least one of a string inverter, a combiner, and a battery.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We claim: 1. A solar panel assembly vertical support structure comprising: a force lateral brace-frame having a first height in a direction parallel to a vertical direction and a first long axis parallel to an X direction perpendicular to the vertical direction and a first width along the first long axis and a first thickness parallel to a Y direction, wherein the Y direction is perpendicular to both the vertical direction and the X direction, wherein the first width is greater than the first thickness, the force lateral brace-frame for counteracting lateral forces parallel to the X direction to create lateral stability; wherein the force lateral brace-frame comprises a main column member; wherein the force lateral brace-frame comprises a component mounting frame; wherein the force lateral brace-frame further comprises an electrical component coupled to the component mounting frame in a screened manner; wherein the force lateral brace-frame comprises a first cladding support coupled to a front face of the force lateral brace-frame, a second cladding support coupled to a rear face of the force lateral brace-frame, a first cladding coupled to the first cladding support and a second cladding coupled to the second cladding support. 2. The solar panel assembly vertical support structure of claim 1 , wherein the first cladding comprises one of a perforated metal, a metal mesh, and a woven fabric, and wherein the first cladding support comprises a door having an attachment mechanism to secure the door in a closed position. 3. The solar panel assembly vertical support structure of claim 1, further comprising: a first side enclosure panel perpendicular to and positioned between the first cladding support and the second cladding support; and a second side enclosure panel perpendicular to and positioned between the first cladding support and the second cladding support and positioned opposite the first side enclosure panel, wherein the first side enclosure panel and second side enclosure panel comprise a vented material. 4. The solar panel assembly vertical support structure of claim 3, further comprising an opening in the force lateral brace-frame suitable for allowing air to flow up from the electrical component and out to the environment surrounding the force lateral brace-frame. 5. The solar panel assembly vertical support structure of claim 1 , wherein the electrical component is a string inverter. 6. The solar panel assembly vertical support structure of claim 1 , wherein the electrical component is one of a string inverter, a combiner, and a battery. 7. A solar energy shade structure, comprising: a solar panel holding structure configured to support and retain a plurality of solar panels; a force lateral brace-frame configured to hold the solar panel holding structure above a surface and comprising a first cladding support coupled to a front face of the force lateral brace-frame, a second cladding support coupled to a rear face of the force lateral brace-frame, and a first cladding coupled to the first cladding support and a second cladding coupled to the second cladding support; an electrical component mounted in a screened manner to the force lateral brace- frame; and a retaining mechanism configured to attach a solar panel of the plurality of solar panels to the solar panel holding structure, wherein the retaining mechanism is accessible from underneath the solar panel for attaching and detaching solar panels. 8. The solar energy shade structure of claim 7, wherein the first cladding comprises one of a perforated metal, a metal mesh, and a woven fabric. 9. The solar energy shade structure of claim 7, further comprising: a first side enclosure panel perpendicular to and positioned between the first cladding support and the second cladding support; a second side enclosure panel perpendicular to and positioned between the first cladding support and the second cladding support and positioned opposite the first side enclosure panel, wherein the first side enclosure panel and second side enclosure panel comprise a vented material; and a top opening for allowing air to pass from an interior portion of the force lateral brace-frame, wherein the interior portion is at least partially located between 

 the first side enclosure panel, the second side enclosure panel, the first cladding and the second cladding. 10. The solar energy shade structure of claim 9, wherein the first cladding support and first cladding comprise a panel having a mechanism suitable to secure the panel in a closed position and to open the panel to provide access to the electrical component. 11. The solar energy shade structure of claim 9, further comprising a component mounting frame for mounting the electrical component between the front face and the rear face. 12. The solar energy shade structure of claim 7, wherein the electrical component comprises at least one of a string inverter and a combiner. 13. A force lateral brace-frame for supporting a solar panel holding structure comprising: a first height in a direction parallel to a vertical direction, a first long axis parallel to an X direction and perpendicular to the vertical direction, a first width along the first long axis, and a first thickness parallel to a Y direction, wherein the Y direction is perpendicular to both the vertical direction and the X direction, wherein the first width is greater than the first thickness, the force lateral brace-frame for counteracting lateral forces parallel to the X direction to create lateral stability, wherein the force lateral brace-frame comprises a main support of the force lateral brace-frame, a front cladding support coupled to the main support and a rear cladding support coupled to the main support, a first cladding coupled to the front cladding support and a second cladding coupled to the rear cladding support, a first side enclosure panel adjacent to and positioned between the front cladding support and the rear cladding support and a second side enclosure panel adjacent to and positioned between the front cladding support and the rear cladding support and positioned opposite the first side enclosure panel, and an electrical component coupled to the force lateral brace-frame. 14. The force lateral brace-frame of claim 13, wherein the first cladding comprises one of a perforated metal, a metal mesh, and a woven fabric, and further comprising an attachment mechanism for opening and closing at least one of the front cladding support and the rear cladding support, for accessing the electrical component. 15. The force lateral brace-frame of claim 13, further comprising a component mounting frame for mounting the electrical component to the force lateral brace-frame, wherein the component mounting frame is coupled between the electrical component and one of the main support. 16. The force lateral brace-frame of claim 13, wherein the electrical component is one of a string inverter and a combiner. 17. A solar energy structure, comprising: a solar panel support structure; a plurality of solar panels supported by the solar panel support structure; a plurality of vertical supports connected to the solar panel support structure for supporting the solar panel support structure elevated above a surface; and a column, located under the solar panel support structure, comprising an electrical component mounted to the column in a screened manner, wherein the electrical component comprises at least one of a string inverter, a combiner, and a battery. 18. The solar energy structure of claim 17, wherein the column has a width, a depth, a height, and a space within the column, wherein the electrical component is mounted to the column in the screened manner by locating the electrical component within the space within the column. 19. The solar energy structure of claim 17, wherein the electrical component is mounted to the column in the screened manner by: a sign, a lighting element, a decorative panel, an advertising panel, or screening the electrical component from view with a panel comprising a breathable mesh cloth. 20. The solar energy structure of claim 17, wherein the electrical component is mounted to the column in the screened manner by locating the electrical component within an outermost portion of the column.
